title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: State of the Union May Be a Trial for ‘Barely Invited’ Justices
items.description: If tradition holds, members of the Supreme Court will attend Trump’s annual speech, just days after ruling against the legality of his tariffs.

items.title: Anthropic claims 3 Chinese companies ripped it off, using its AI tools to train their models: ‘How the turn tables’
items.description: Anthropic, which settled a copyright claim by thousands of authors for $1.5 billion months ago, claims the Chinese firms used "distillation" technique...

items.title: A Blizzard With Record Snowfall for Parts of the Northeast
items.description: At the storm’s peak, well over half a million homes and businesses in the Northeast were without power.

items.title: Steve Jobs adopted a no ‘bozos’ policy and said the best managers are those who never wanted the job—here are his 3 best management tips
items.description: Hiring only for “professional management” doesn’t work, Steve Jobs said.

items.title: Meta Announces Major Chips-for-Stock Deal With AMD
items.description: The multibillion-dollar deal is AMD’s latest move to catch up to Nvidia in the lucrative world of selling artificial intelligence chips.

items.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy says sucking up to your boss won’t earn their trust
items.description: Want to earn the trust of your boss and peers? Sucking up to them won’t do the trick, says Amazon’s CEO Andy Jassy.

items.title: The AI leadership reckoning is here
items.description: Your CEO is in danger and generational change is afoot.

items.title: ‘It’s going to be painful for a lot of people’: Software engineers could go extinct this year, says Claude Code creator
items.description: Boris Cherny says Claude Code has been writing all of his code since November.

items.title: Robert Carradine, Actor Who Played the Father in ‘Lizzie McGuire,’ Dies at 71
items.description: A member of a renowned acting dynasty, he also earned fame for his role in “Revenge of the Nerds.” His family said he struggled with bipolar disorder.

items.title: Supreme Court Sides With Couple in Case Involving Baby Food Sold at Whole Foods
items.description: The court agreed to revive a lawsuit by a Texas couple who claimed that tainted baby food purchased at Whole Foods had sickened their young son.

items.title: Airlines Add Flights After Storm and Snow in Northeast Cause Cancellations
items.description: The major U.S. airlines said they would add more flights in airports that serve cities like Boston, New York and Philadelphia.

items.title: Mandelson’s Arrest Adds to Scandal That Has Rattled Starmer and Labour Party
items.description: The arrest of the British former envoy to Washington, long a key figure in the Labour Party, deepened a scandal that has led to calls for Prime Minist...

items.title: Russia Moves Closer to Banning Telegram App, Saying It Foments Terrorism
items.description: News of an investigation into Pavel Durov, the app’s billionaire founder, deepened a crackdown on what remains of the free Russian internet.

items.title: Mexico Is Caught Between Trump and the Cartels
items.description: President Trump has demanded President Claudia Sheinbaum confront the cartels. The killing of “El Mencho” suggests it might be working — but could com...

items.title: After Six Decades of the War on Drugs, What Works?
items.description: The U.S. and its allies have spilled blood and treasure to kill drug lords and defeat cartels, but the drugs keep coming and the new groups are more v...
